proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving `treatment_enzalutamide` have a higher probability of `objective_response` than patients not receiving `treatment_enzalutamide`, after adjustment for disease status, biomarkers, selected labs, metastatic sites, and other treatments.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted linear-probability model: enzalutamide was associated with a 0.202 absolute increase in objective response probability (p=<1e-300). Unadjusted response was 0.361 on treatment vs 0.159 off treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `mcrpc=1` have a lower probability of `objective_response` than patients with `mcrpc=0`, after adjustment for treatments, biomarkers, selected labs, and metastatic sites.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted model: mCRPC was associated with a -0.193 absolute response-probability difference (p=<1e-300); unadjusted response was 0.154 with mCRPC vs 0.346 without mCRPC.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `brca2_mutation=1` have a lower probability of `objective_response` than patients with `brca2_mutation=0`, after adjustment for treatments and clinical covariates.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted model: BRCA2 mutation carried a -0.102 absolute response-probability difference (p=<1e-300); unadjusted response was 0.150 vs 0.250.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `ar_v7_positive=1` have a lower probability of `objective_response` than patients with `ar_v7_positive=0`, after adjustment for treatments and clinical covariates.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted model: AR-V7 positivity carried a -0.100 absolute response-probability difference (p=<1e-300); unadjusted response was 0.160 vs 0.260.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `msi_high=1` have a lower probability of `objective_response` than patients with `msi_high=0`, after adjustment for treatments and clinical covariates.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted model: MSI-high status carried a -0.063 absolute response-probability difference (p=2.34e-9); unadjusted response was 0.176 vs 0.242.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `psma_high=1` have a higher probability of `objective_response` than patients with `psma_high=0`, after adjustment for treatments and clinical covariates.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted model: PSMA-high status was not associated with higher response; the adjusted difference was -0.005 (p=0.200).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A one-standard-deviation higher `ecog_ps` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`, after adjustment for treatments, biomarkers, selected labs, and metastatic sites.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted model: each SD higher ECOG performance status was associated with a -0.034 absolute response-probability difference (p=<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A one-standard-deviation higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`, after adjustment for treatments, biomarkers, selected labs, and metastatic sites.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted model: each SD higher six-month weight loss was associated with a -0.022 absolute response-probability difference (p=<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A one-standard-deviation higher `albumin_g_dl` is associated with a higher probability of `objective_response`, after adjustment for treatments, biomarkers, selected labs, and metastatic sites.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted model: each SD higher albumin was associated with a 0.010 absolute response-probability difference (p=1.54e-8).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients at the 75th percentile of `psa_ng_ml` have a lower probability of `objective_response` than patients at the 25th percentile of `psa_ng_ml`.

analyses.result_summary: Univariable linear-probability model: moving from the 25th percentile PSA (6.46 ng/mL) to the 75th percentile (41.28 ng/mL) changed response probability by -0.014 (p=<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A one-standard-deviation higher `crp_mg_l` is associated with a lower probability of `objective_response`, after adjustment for treatments, biomarkers, selected labs, and metastatic sites.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted model: each SD higher CRP was associated with a -0.009 absolute response-probability difference (p=2.74e-8).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients at the 75th percentile of `nlr` have a lower probability of `objective_response` than patients at the 25th percentile of `nlr`.

analyses.result_summary: Univariable model: moving from the 25th percentile NLR (2.06) to the 75th percentile (4.37) changed response probability by -0.001 (p=0.611), so this inflammation hypothesis was not supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `liver_mets=1` have a lower probability of `objective_response` than patients with `liver_mets=0`, after adjustment for treatments, biomarkers, selected labs, and other metastatic indicators.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted model: liver metastases had a -0.003 absolute response-probability difference (p=0.562), providing no evidence of an independent liver-metastasis response decrement.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `treatment_enzalutamide` and `objective_response` is smaller in patients with `mcrpc=1` than in patients with `mcrpc=0`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction model: the enzalutamide-by-mCRPC interaction was -0.430 on the response-probability scale (p=<1e-300), indicating major attenuation of enzalutamide-associated response in mCRPC.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with `mcrpc=0`, `treatment_enzalutamide` is associated with a higher probability of `objective_response` than no `treatment_enzalutamide`.

analyses.result_summary: Non-mCRPC subgroup: response was 0.610 with enzalutamide vs 0.169 without it, an absolute difference of 0.440 (p=<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with `mcrpc=1`, `treatment_enzalutamide` is associated with a higher probability of `objective_response` than no `treatment_enzalutamide`.

analyses.result_summary: mCRPC subgroup: response was 0.158 with enzalutamide vs 0.150 without it, an absolute difference of 0.008 (p=0.086), so the subgroup-specific hypothesis was not statistically supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association between `treatment_enzalutamide` and `objective_response` is smaller in patients with `ar_v7_positive=1` than in patients with `ar_v7_positive=0`, even when the enzalutamide-by-`mcrpc` interaction is modeled.

analyses.result_summary: Joint interaction model including enzalutamide-by-mCRPC and enzalutamide-by-AR-V7: the AR-V7 interaction term was -0.241 (p=<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association between `treatment_abiraterone` and `objective_response` is smaller in patients with `ar_v7_positive=1` than in patients with `ar_v7_positive=0`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction model: the abiraterone-by-AR-V7 interaction was 0.010 (p=0.274), not supporting AR-V7-specific abiraterone attenuation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association between `treatment_olaparib` and `objective_response` is more positive in patients with `brca2_mutation=1` than in patients with `brca2_mutation=0`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction model: the olaparib-by-BRCA2 interaction was -0.035 (p=0.050). In the BRCA2-mutated subgroup, olaparib response was 0.121 vs 0.153 off olaparib (RD -0.032, p=0.034).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association between `treatment_pembrolizumab` and `objective_response` is more positive in patients with `msi_high=1` than in patients with `msi_high=0`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction model: the pembrolizumab-by-MSI-high interaction was -0.011 (p=0.811). In MSI-high patients, pembrolizumab response was 0.177 vs 0.176 off treatment (RD 0.001, p=0.978).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association between `treatment_lu177_psma` and `objective_response` is more positive in patients with `psma_high=1` than in patients with `psma_high=0`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction model: the Lu-177-PSMA-by-PSMA-high interaction was -0.008 (p=0.418). In PSMA-high patients, Lu-177-PSMA response was 0.238 vs 0.239 off treatment (RD -0.001, p=0.868).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association between `treatment_docetaxel` and `objective_response` is more positive in patients with `visceral_mets=1` than in patients with `visceral_mets=0`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction model: the docetaxel-by-visceral-metastases interaction was 0.003 (p=0.736), providing no evidence of a visceral-metastasis-specific docetaxel response advantage.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association between `treatment_pembrolizumab` and `objective_response` is less positive in patients with `autoimmune_disease=1` than in patients with `autoimmune_disease=0`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction model: the pembrolizumab-by-autoimmune-disease interaction was 0.007 (p=0.851), not supporting autoimmune-disease attenuation in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `met_exon14_skipping=1` have a lower probability of `objective_response` than patients with `met_exon14_skipping=0`.

analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted comparison: MET exon 14 skipping had response 0.222 vs 0.241 without the alteration, an absolute difference of -0.019 (p=0.088), which was not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients at the 75th percentile of `age_years` have a lower probability of `objective_response` than patients at the 25th percentile of `age_years`.

analyses.result_summary: Univariable model: moving from the 25th percentile age (58.3 years) to the 75th percentile (71.8 years) changed response probability by 5.88e-4 (p=0.820), refuting an age-response gradient.
